required_slots.noise_descriptor: squeal/squeak/chirp vs grind — separates near-worn pads (inspection soon) from metal-on-metal (urgent, rotors at risk)

required_slots.onset_timing: only while braking vs constant even off the brake — a constant squeal that stops WHEN braking points at the wear indicator dragging; noise unrelated to braking may be a belt

required_slots.location_axle: front or rear if the customer can tell — preps the advisor and the estimate

required_slots.started_when: how long / getting worse — urgency and whether rotors are likely involved yet

confusables.q: Is it a high-pitched squeak or squeal, or more of a rough metal-on-metal grinding?
Does the noise happen when you press the brakes, or when you turn the steering wheel?
Do you hear it when braking, or when going over bumps and dips?
Does it only happen when you're braking, or is the noise there any time the engine is running?

notes: Squeal is usually the pad wear indicator contacting the rotor — pads near end of life, service soon [S1]. Includes chirp/screech/whistle framed around braking. Does NOT include grinding (own entry, urgent), squeal on turning (steering/belt), or bump-triggered squeaks (suspension). Customers often write "breaks"/"squeeking" — keep misspelled variants. If squeal has already progressed to a red BRAKE light, route brake_system_red_light instead.

required_slots.noise_descriptor: grind/scrape/crunch confirms metal-on-metal vs squeal — changes urgency and scope (pads + rotors)

required_slots.onset_timing: every stop vs only reversing vs only hard stops — reverse-only grinding is often rear shoes/backing plate

required_slots.location_axle: front vs rear / which corner — advisor prep

required_slots.started_when: how long it's been grinding — longer = more rotor damage likely

confusables.q: Is it a rough grinding like metal rubbing metal, or a high-pitched squeal?
Does the noise happen when you press the brakes, or when you drive over bumps?
Do you hear it only when braking, or does it hum/grind constantly as you drive and change with speed?

notes: Grinding = friction material worn away, backing plate contacting the rotor — rotor damage accrues with every stop [S2]. Treat as urgent. Includes scraping/crunching/growling tied to brake application (including in reverse). Does NOT include speed-dependent hum (bearing) or dragging noises not tied to the pedal. Customers frequently self-diagnose ("I think I need brakes") — keep the symptom, ignore the self-diagnosis for routing.

required_slots.speed_band: highway-speed braking vs all speeds — classic rotor thickness-variation pattern is worst from speed

required_slots.onset_timing: only when braking (must be brake-triggered to stay in brakes)

required_slots.started_when: gradual vs after recent brake work — recent-service pulsation routes the advisor differently

Keep both this pedal-feel entry and the whole-car sibling; the discriminator is WHERE the customer feels it [S4]. Does NOT include ABS pedal buzz during a hard emergency stop on gravel/ice (normal ABS engagement — note in conversation, not a fault).

Brake-only vibration stays in brakes (live rule: NOT suspension). Severe shake + pull together can be a sticking caliper — advisor note.

required_slots.pedal_feel: hard/high-effort — the booster-side signature vs soft/sinking hydraulic signatures

required_slots.onset_timing: always vs first stops only vs after repeated quick stops — a check-valve leak gets stiffer with rapid repeats

notes: High-effort pedal is most often booster vacuum loss — cracked vacuum hose, torn diaphragm, or failed check valve [S6]. Stopping distance increases substantially, hence the flag. Does NOT include the normal firm pedal with the engine OFF, or hybrid/EV regen feel complaints (advisor conversation).

required_slots.diesel_or_gas: a brief diesel puff under load can be near-normal; sustained black smoke is not

required_slots.running_quality_and_cel: rough running / check engine light alongside

confusables.q: Is it sooty black with a gas smell, or blue-gray with a burning-oil smell?
Black, or thick white?
Do you see actual black smoke, or is it mainly a rotten-egg smell from the exhaust?

notes: Rich mixture — too much fuel or not enough air (injectors, sensors, clogged air filter) [S2]. Drivable but wastes fuel and can damage the catalytic converter; book promptly. Diesel-vs-gas matters: diesels puff black under load semi-normally, so sustained/idle black smoke is the bookable signal there.

display: Smoke or steam from under the hood
anchors: smoke coming from under the hood
steam pouring out from under the hood and it smells sweet
SAW SMOKE COMING FROM THE GRILLE
white steam coming out of the engine bay after i park

hissing and steam from under my hood but the gauge says its not overheating

something is smoking under the hood so i pulled over right away

required_slots.smell: sweet = coolant boil-over/spray; greasy burnt = oil on the manifold; sharp plastic = electrical (different domain) — the smell routes it

required_slots.temperature_gauge_state: hot gauge or overheat message = the cooling side; normal gauge shifts odds to oil/electrical

required_slots.still_driving: visible under-hood smoke warrants pull-over-and-assess advice; tow if it continues [S3]

required_slots.steam_vs_smoke_as_seen: billowing white vapor (steam) vs gray/black smoke — customers use the words interchangeably

required_slots.fluid_loss: coolant or oil level dropping ties it to the source

confusables.q: Is the temperature gauge reading hot along with the smoke, or is the gauge normal?
Does it smell sweet or oily, or like sharp burning plastic/wiring?
Can you actually see smoke or steam, or is it just the smell?

notes: Anything visibly smoking/steaming under the hood gets stop-and-assess advice [S3]. The smell is the router: sweet+hot = coolant (this domain), greasy = oil on the exhaust (this domain), acrid plastic = electrical (cross-domain — electrical fires escalate to 'get away from the vehicle if it worsens'). Smoke that appeared right after a repair elsewhere is also a situational/after-recent-service case.

display: Squeal / chirp / whine from under the hood (belt or accessory)
anchors: loud squealing noise when i start the car in the morning, goes away after a minute
high pitched squeal from under the hood when i accelerate
car screeches when i first start it up, especially when its cold out
squealing noise that gets louder when i turn the steering wheel
SQUEALING NOISE UNDER HOOD ON STARTUP
chirping sound from the engine that speeds up when i rev it
belt squeeling really bad when the AC kicks on
whining noise from the engine that gets higher when i give it gas
sounds like a bird chirping under the hood, been going on for weeks
serpentine belt screaming on cold mornings till the car warms up
loud squeal when i rev the engine even sitting in park
engine makes a whine that follows the rpms not the speed

required_slots.noise_descriptor: squeal/screech vs rhythmic chirp vs steady whine — squeal = belt slipping (tension/glazing), chirp = misalignment or a failing idler/tensioner pulley, whine = a bearing (idler, tensioner, alternator, PS pump) [G1]

required_slots.onset_timing: cold start / accelerating / A/C on / turning the wheel — each loads the belt differently and localizes the accessory (A/C-engage squeal = compressor load; turning = PS pump load) [G1][G2]

required_slots.engine_running: does it follow engine REVS even parked in Park/Neutral — engine-tied noise is the belt/accessory signature; road-speed-tied noise is wheels/tires/driveline

required_slots.warning_light_named: battery light or temperature climbing with it — a slipping/shredded belt can stop the alternator or water pump; escalate

required_slots.started_when: days vs months, and whether it's getting longer/louder — a brief cold-morning squeal that's now constant is a belt near the end

confusables.q: Does it only happen when you're braking, or is the noise there any time the engine is running?
Does the noise only happen while turning the wheel, or is it there any time the engine is running?
Does the sound follow your road speed even coasting in neutral, or does it follow the engine revs?
Does the noise change when you press or release the CLUTCH pedal, or does it stay the same and just follow engine revs?
Is it an airy whistle/hiss, or more of a squeal/chirp like a belt (especially on cold start or when turning)?
Does the noise change with the FAN speed and vent buttons, or is it a squeal from under the hood when the A/C kicks on?
Is a warning light on with the squeal, like the battery light? (A slipping or broken belt can be the cause.)

notes: CANONICAL entry for the biggest coverage gap — five domains referenced this concern under five different names (belt_squeal_under_hood, squealing_belt_noise, belt_squeal_or_whine, belt_or_underhood_squeal, high_pitched_whining_under_the_hood); synthesis should rewrite all of those `vs:` aliases to this slug. RPM-linked and engine-tied, NOT brake-linked (brakes domain) and NOT road-speed-tied (bearing/tire/driveline). Squeal-on-turning overlaps the PS system: if the whine is loudest ONLY while turning and the reservoir is low, that is the hydraulic-PS starving picture — route the steering/leak entries instead [G3]; if it's there at idle and rises with revs regardless of steering, it stays here. Escalation: squeal + battery light or temp rising = belt failing in real time (charging/water pump at risk) — advise promptly. safety_flag stays none for the plain squeal; the escalation rides on the co-reported light.

display: Shakes at idle in gear (smooths in Neutral/Park)
anchors: car shakes when im stopped at a red light in drive but stops if i put it in neutral
vibrates really bad at stop lights, smooths out in park
whole car trembles sitting in drive with my foot on the brake
shaking at idle but the engine sounds fine
CAR SHAKES WHEN STOPPED IN DRIVE
feel a thud when i shift into drive and then it shakes at every light
shakes worse at idle when the ac is on
car shudders sitting still but my mechanic says the engine runs smooth
vibration through the seat and steering wheel when stopped in gear
clunk under the hood when i take off and it vibrates at stoplights
idles rough in gear, fine in park, no check engine light

required_slots.gear_position_effect: PROPOSED-NEW slot — shakes in Drive/Reverse at a stop but smooths in Neutral/Park; the load-on-the-mounts signature and the single most separating fact [G4]

required_slots.noise_descriptor: any thump/clunk when shifting into gear or accelerating — the engine moving on a broken mount [G4]

required_slots.onset_timing: only stopped in gear vs also while driving — mount shake concentrates at idle-in-gear; if it bucks while accelerating it's misfire territory

required_slots.started_when: gradual (rubber ages) vs after an impact or recent engine/trans work

confusables.q: Does the engine itself sound like it's struggling or sputtering, or does the engine sound normal and the car just vibrates (worn engine mount)?
Does it also shake while sitting still in Park or at a light, or only when moving?
Does it shake while sitting STILL at a light, or mainly while accelerating / around 40-50 mph?

notes: The vibration-domain twin of performance/rough_idle_or_shaking_at_a_stop — ENG's rough-idle entry and BST's constant-vibration entry both point here (confusable-matrix alias: shaking_at_idle_motor_mounts). Engine SOUNDS normal, body shakes; putting it in Neutral lessening the shake is the classic worn-mount self-test, and A/C-on making it worse fits (extra idle load) [G4]. Includes the shift-into-gear thud/clunk sibling. Does NOT include a sputtering/stumbling engine (rough idle — performance), shake that dies with the engine warming (cold-start enrichment), or shudder while accelerating (transmission_shudder / cv_axle_accel_shudder). Worn mounts also aggravate belt misalignment and torque steer — advisor note, not a routing exit.

anchors: burning plastic smell coming from my dash
smells like melting wires under the hood
electrical burning smell when i turn the headlights on
BURNING SMELL FROM THE VENTS, SMELLS LIKE PLASTIC
weird fishy burning smell in the car, cant find where its coming from
smelled something electrical burning and then my radio died
sharp chemical plastic smell when i run the heater
burning smell near the fuse box and a fuse keeps blowing
burnt plastic smell after the car sat running in the driveway
acrid burning smell inside the car, made my eyes sting
melted plastic smell from the dashboard and now the power windows dont work

required_slots.smell_descriptor: sharp plastic/electrical/fishy vs oily vs sweet vs rubber — the smell family is the router; hot electrical insulation reads as acrid plastic or fishy [G5]

required_slots.sound_or_smoke_location_zone: dash/vents/fuse box vs under hood vs a wheel — inside-the-cabin electrical smell points at wiring/blower circuits; one hot wheel is a dragging brake

required_slots.onset_timing: tied to using one accessory (headlights, heater, seat warmer) vs any time running — accessory-tied smell localizes the circuit

required_slots.drivable_state: smelled once vs active/strengthening now, any visible smoke — active electrical smell is a stop-driving conversation

required_slots.warning_light_behavior: anything else electrical acting up with it (dead accessory, blowing fuses, flickering) — smell + a dead circuit is the confirmed-overheat picture

confusables.q: Is it a greasy/oily burnt smell, or a sharp plastic/electrical burning smell?
Does it smell sweet or oily, or like sharp burning plastic/wiring?
Was there any burning/melting-plastic smell when it quit?
Is it a burning-rubber smell coming from one of the wheels, or a sharp plastic/electrical smell strongest under the hood or inside the car?

notes: The electrical-fire escalation lane — FLU and ELE both referenced it (ELE alias: burning_electrical_smell) with no anchor home. Overheating insulation and shorting wiring produce the acrid melting-plastic (sometimes fishy) smell and can escalate to an actual fire; the advice is stop, switch off, don't keep driving it to the appointment [G5]. Smell + a simultaneously-dead accessory or repeat-blowing fuse strengthens the picture (keep the symptom pair in ONE booking). Does NOT include burning OIL (greasy, oil-leak entry), sweet coolant steam, burning RUBBER — a rubber smell at a wheel is a hot/dragging brake (smoke_or_burning_smell_from_a_wheel, into which the matrix folded burning_rubber_hot_brake_smell), and a rubber smell from under the hood is a slipping/shredding belt (belt_squeal_underhood_whine) — or the first-heat-of-the-season dusty smell from the heater (benign, advisor conversation if mentioned alone).

display: Rotten-egg / sulfur smell at the battery (overcharging)
anchors: rotten egg smell under the hood
battery smells like sulfur and the case looks swollen
eggy smell coming from the engine bay, not the exhaust
smells like rotten eggs near the battery and i could hear it hissing
battery is boiling and stinks like sulfur
ROTTEN EGG SMELL UNDER HOOD, battery covered in white crust
sulfur smell after driving and the battery is hot to the touch
new battery keeps dying and now theres a rotten egg smell up front
acid smell around the battery, the top of it looks wet
sulphur smell under the hood right after i shut the car off

required_slots.sound_or_smoke_location_zone: under the hood AT the battery vs at the tailpipe — the battery-vs-catalytic-converter split; wet/crusty/swollen/hot battery case confirms the battery side [G10]

required_slots.smell_descriptor: rotten egg/sulfur — shared with the converter; acid smell + hissing points battery

required_slots.warning_light_named: battery/charging light with it — overcharging is a charging-system fault (voltage regulator), not just a bad battery [G10]

required_slots.recent_action: recent battery replacement or jump start — a fresh battery boiling points harder at overcharging

required_slots.drivable_state: actively hissing/hot/swollen now vs a whiff after driving — active venting = stop, ventilate, don't lean over the battery

confusables.q: Is the smell strongest at the tailpipe while driving, or under the hood near the battery?
Is the main concern the warning light, or an actual rotten-egg smell / hot swollen battery under the hood?
Do you see smoke or steam, or is it mainly the rotten-egg smell around the battery?

notes: Distinct from the catalytic-converter rotten-egg entry (exhaust side, smell while driving, ENG domain): this is hydrogen sulfide venting from boiling battery electrolyte — overcharging from a failed voltage regulator (or a battery at end of life). The vented gas is toxic AND flammable and a hot/swollen case can rupture; advise shutting the car off and not driving it in [G10]. Overcharging is a charging-system diagnosis (regulator/alternator), so the battery-light co-report matters — the fix is rarely just a battery. Does NOT include plain white terminal corrosion with no smell (maintenance/battery-test request) or sulfur smell only at the exhaust (rotten_egg_sulfur_smell).

display: Rattle / metallic buzz underneath the car
anchors: rattling noise under the car when its idling
metal rattle underneath at stop lights, goes away when i rev it
sounds like a loose piece of metal buzzing under the car
rattle under the car right around 2000 rpm
something rattling under my car, sounds like a can full of marbles
RATTLING UNDERNEATH WHEN IDLING
buzzing vibration under the floor at certain engine speeds
i think my heat shield is rattling, tinny rattle under the car
rattles under the car when its cold, quiets down once it warms up
loose rattle down by the exhaust when the car is just sitting there running
rattle underneath when i let off the gas coasting

required_slots.noise_descriptor: thin/tinny metallic rattle or buzz — vs a deep one-corner clunk (suspension) or an overall louder exhaust note (leak)

required_slots.onset_timing: at idle / a specific RPM band / on rev-and-release — a loose shield resonates at particular engine speeds and comes and goes with revs [G6]

required_slots.sound_or_smoke_location_zone: under the middle/rear (exhaust line) vs one corner vs under the hood — underneath-along-the-exhaust is the shield/hanger zone

required_slots.started_when: after an exhaust repair, bottoming out, or gradual (rusted fasteners) [G6]

confusables.q: Is it a solid clunk from one corner over bumps, or a lighter metallic rattle from underneath (like a loose heat shield)?
Is the exhaust sound itself louder, or is it more of a metal rattle or buzz underneath at certain engine speeds?
Is it a rhythmic tick from up in the engine, or a looser metallic rattle from underneath the car?

notes: Canonical slug per 01-taxonomy.md (confusable-matrix alias: rattle_underneath_heat_shield). The classic presentation is a corroded/loose exhaust heat shield chattering at idle or one RPM band — annoying, cheap to fix, and NOT urgent by itself [G6]; loose exhaust hangers and clamps present the same way. Bump-triggered corner clunks are suspension (BST); a raspier overall exhaust note is a leak (ENG); a rattle INSIDE the cabin (dash/door panel) is a trim/advisor conversation, not this. A catalytic-converter internal rattle (marbles at rev-and-release) also lands here — the booking is the same look-underneath inspection; advisor sorts shield vs converter on the lift.

display: Pulls to one side only when accelerating (torque steer)
anchors: steering wheel pulls to the right when i floor it
car yanks to one side when i accelerate hard
pulls left only when im getting on the highway on-ramp hard
when i step on the gas the wheel jerks to one side, drives straight otherwise
car darts right under hard acceleration
PULLS TO THE SIDE WHEN ACCELERATING
wheel tugs in my hands when i punch the gas, fine when cruising
veers right when taking off from a light, doesnt do it braking or cruising
feels like the car steers itself when i accelerate hard, ok at steady speed
torque steer got way worse lately, pulls hard when i give it gas

required_slots.onset_timing: ONLY under hard throttle — the defining fact; releases/straightens when you lift. Cruise pulls and brake pulls are different entries

required_slots.pull_direction: left or right — consistent direction under power

required_slots.started_when: always did it a little vs recently got worse — some torque steer is normal in powerful FWD cars; a CHANGE points at worn parts [G7]

required_slots.tire_state: uneven pressure or mismatched/worn front tires amplify it — cheapest thing to rule out [G7]

required_slots.noise_descriptor: any clunk or clicking with it — clunk-on-throttle suggests a mount; clicking on turns is a CV joint

confusables.q: Does it pull only while you're accelerating hard, or does it drift to one side even cruising at steady speed?
Does it pull when you're on the gas, or when you're pressing the brakes?
Does the car PULL to one side under acceleration, or mainly shudder/vibrate when accelerating?

notes: Taxonomy alias: torque_steer_pull_on_acceleration. BST's pull entries explicitly exclude this to the performance/driveline side; this is the entry they hand to. Mild pull under hard throttle is inherent to unequal-length-half-shaft FWD layouts; a pull that's NEW or worsening implicates worn control-arm bushings, engine/trans mounts, CV joints, or a tire/pressure split — bookable as a front-end inspection [G7]. Throttle-on vs throttle-off is the routing hinge: cruise pull = steady_drift_while_cruising (alignment/tire), brake pull = pulling_only_when_braking (caliper). Cross-link: worn mounts also show up as shaking_at_idle_while_stopped — same underlying part, keep both symptoms in one booking if co-reported.

display: Transmission hunts / keeps shifting at steady speed
anchors: transmission keeps shifting up and down on the highway
cant decide what gear it wants on the interstate
rpms keep jumping up and down at 65 like it keeps changing gears
shifts in and out of overdrive constantly on hills
transmision hunting between gears when i tow my camper
keeps downshifting and upshifting at a steady speed, drives me crazy
TRANSMISSION SHIFTING CONSTANTLY ON HIGHWAY
feels like it shifts every few seconds going up the grade with cruise on
gear hunting between 60 and 70, back and forth nonstop
rpm bounces between 2000 and 2500 just cruising, like its stuck between gears

required_slots.speed_band: steady highway cruise (and whether cruise control is on) — the defining context; the RPM swing at CONSTANT road speed is what makes it hunting, not surging

required_slots.onset_timing: only on grades/towing vs on flat ground too — some up/downshifting on a grade or towing is normal strategy; constant flat-ground hunting is the fault picture [G8]

required_slots.recent_action: recent transmission fluid service / how old the fluid is — degraded or low fluid and sticky solenoids are common culprits [G8]

required_slots.warning_light_named: check engine light with it — TCC and shift-solenoid faults commonly set codes

required_slots.started_when: gradual vs sudden; any shudder or slip with it

confusables.q: Do the RPMs swing while you're cruising at a steady speed on the highway (like it can't pick a gear), or while you're stopped / at idle?
Does it feel like actual gear changes back and forth, or do the revs flare up without the car pulling any harder (slipping)?
Is the problem that it shifts too OFTEN at steady speed, or that each shift bangs/jerks hard?

notes: ENG's surging entry hands the at-cruise version here ("can't pick a gear on the highway"). Busy shifting climbing a grade or towing is often normal shift strategy (tow/haul mode exists for it); persistent hunting on flat ground points at the torque-converter-clutch applying/releasing repeatedly, shift solenoids, degraded fluid, or a speed-sensor input [G8]. A light-throttle shudder while it hunts strengthens the TCC read (see transmission_shudder). Distinct from engine surging at idle (RPM swings while stopped — performance domain) and from slipping (revs rise, no shift actually happens).

display: Shifter stuck in Park / won't shift out of Park
anchors: cant get my car out of park
shifter is stuck in park even with my foot on the brake
gear shift wont move out of park this morning
stuck in park and my brake lights arent working either
have to use the little override slot by the shifter to get it out of park
STUCK IN PARK WONT SHIFT
car starts fine but the shifter wont budge out of P
shift lever locks up in park randomly, some days its fine
press the brake and the shifter still wont release
wife is stranded at the store, car will not come out of park

required_slots.brake_lights_working: PROPOSED-NEW slot — do the brake lights come on when the pedal is pressed? Dead brake lights + stuck in Park = the brake-light switch, the most common cause [G9]

required_slots.onset_timing: every time vs intermittent vs only parked nose-up on a hill — parked-on-an-incline strain on the park pawl is the mechanical (non-electrical) look-alike

required_slots.drivable_state: are they stranded right now — the shift-lock override slot can free it to drive in; otherwise this books as a come-to-you/tow conversation [G9]

required_slots.started_when: sudden this morning vs building for weeks; anything else electrical with it

confusables.q: Will the shift lever physically not come OUT of Park, or does it move to Drive and the car still doesn't go?
Is the shifter itself locked in Park, or does it shift fine and the car won't roll (parking brake stuck)?
Are the brake lights out AND the shifter stuck in Park, or just a bulb out? (Both together are usually one switch.)

notes: Brake-shift interlock: ignition on + brake pressed releases the shifter via the interlock solenoid, and the brake-light SWITCH feeds that signal — so a failed switch presents as "stuck in Park," very often WITH dead brake lights (one fault, two symptoms — keep them in one booking; dead brake lights alone are also a rear-end-collision risk worth prompt advice) [G9]. Other causes: the interlock solenoid itself, an open ignition-switch input. Categorized electrical (body-electrical) per the taxonomy; PWT's wont_move_or_no_gear hands the lever-won't-leave-Park case here. Does NOT include a manual car that won't go into gear (wont_go_into_gear_manual) or a lever that moves but the trans doesn't engage (delayed_engagement / wont_move_or_no_gear). Mention the shift-lock override slot exists so a stranded customer can get it in.

anchors: headlights are all foggy and yellow, want them cleaned up
cant see at night, my headlight lenses are cloudy
headlights look hazy and dull but the bulbs are fine
want my headlights restored, theyre all yellowed
headlight covers are oxidized and foggy
HEADLIGHTS CLOUDY CANT SEE AT NIGHT
the lenses on my headlights are so hazy the light barely comes through
headlights yellowing bad, got flagged at inspection for it
do you guys do headlight restoration? mine are fogged over
plastic over my headlights looks frosted and my night vision is terrible

required_slots.started_when: gradual haze over years (UV oxidation — restoration) vs sudden fogging INSIDE the lens (moisture intrusion — seal/housing, different job)

required_slots.warning_light_behavior: confirm the bulbs actually light and don't flicker — dull-but-steady through a hazy lens is optical; changing brightness is electrical

required_slots.drivable_state: how bad is night driving for them — severe haze is a real visibility hazard and bumps urgency [G11]

confusables.q: Do the lights actually change brightness, or are they just always dull/yellowed looking (hazy lenses)?
Is the bulb out, or do the lights work but look dull/yellowed through hazy lenses?

notes: Restoration/appearance REQUEST, not an electrical fault — ELE referenced it twice as the optical look-alike (ELE alias: headlights_cloudy_or_always_dim). UV oxidation of the polycarbonate lens is the cause; severe oxidation can cut usable light output dramatically (up to ~80%), so "can't see at night" complaints with working bulbs are legitimately safety-adjacent even though the fix is a lens restoration + UV sealant rather than parts [G11]. Moisture/condensation INSIDE the housing is a different repair (seal or housing replacement) — the inside-vs-outside question is worth the advisor's time. New-bulbs-didn't-help is a classic tell for this entry.

anchors: cruise control stopped working
cruise wont set when i press the button
cruise control light comes on but it wont hold my speed
cruise quit working, the buttons do nothing
CRUISE CONTROL NOT WORKING
set the cruise and it kicks right back off
cruise control hasnt worked since my brake lights were fixed
cruise wont engage at all, no light nothing
cruse control dead on my truck, use it every day for work

required_slots.onset_timing: won't engage at all vs sets-then-drops immediately — drop-on-set points at the brake-light-switch input; fully dead points at fuse/switch [G12]

required_slots.brake_lights_working: PROPOSED-NEW slot — brake lights stuck ON or misadjusted after brake work will keep cruise from engaging; the classic after-brake-service story [G12]

required_slots.recent_action: recent brake work, battery change, or switch replacement — the top onset stories

required_slots.warning_light_named: CEL or other lights with it — many systems disable cruise while a fault code is active

confusables.q: Is it specifically the cruise control that quit, or a different accessory?
Is it the basic set-a-speed cruise, or the adaptive kind that keeps distance from the car ahead (camera/radar system)?

notes: The dedicated home for plain (non-adaptive) cruise complaints — ELE's notes said "ask" but had no anchor. Top causes: blown fuse and the brake-light switch (cruise reads that input to disengage; a stuck-on or misadjusted switch blocks engagement — hence the after-brake-work story); older vacuum-actuated systems add vacuum leaks; speed-sensor faults also disable it [G12]. ADAPTIVE cruise / radar-camera complaints route to the ADAS domain (driver_assist_warning_or_malfunction) — the adaptive-or-basic question is the fork. Many cars also suspend cruise while ANY powertrain code is active, so cruise-dead + CEL books as the CEL diagnostic first.

anchors: my remote start stopped working
remote starter wont start the car anymore, lock and unlock still work
aftermarket remote start quit after i got a new battery put in
press start on the fob twice and nothing happens, the lights just flash
remote start worked all winter and now nothing
REMOTE START NOT WORKING
the remote starter thing wont start my car, think it got put in valet mode?
remote start hasnt worked since the shop had the hood open
remote car starter clicks the locks but wont turn the engine over

required_slots.recent_action: recent battery change, service visit (hood open), or fob battery — battery disconnects drop programming and hood-open service trips the hood pin [G13]

required_slots.onset_timing: lock/unlock still work but start doesn't = the valet-mode / hood-pin signature; everything dead = fob battery or antenna [G13]

required_slots.started_when: factory system vs aftermarket install (and roughly when it was installed) — aftermarket routes to the installer conversation if we didn't install it

confusables.q: Do lock and unlock still work from the fob (just remote START doesn't), or is the whole fob dead?
Does the car start normally with the key/button from inside, and only the REMOTE start fails?

notes: Aftermarket-dominant complaint. If the car starts fine from the driver's seat and only remote start fails, the big three are: valet mode accidentally enabled (lock/unlock work, start doesn't — often exits via a lock+trunk button combo), a broken/corroded hood-pin switch (safety input that reports the hood open; parking lights flash but no crank), and lost programming after a battery disconnect [G13]. Booking is a diagnostic; if it's a third-party install we didn't do, set the expectation that the original installer may be the right stop. If the car ALSO won't start with the key, route the no_start entries instead — that's a car problem, not a remote-start problem.

display: Gauges / instrument cluster dead or erratic
anchors: none of my gauges work
dashboard gauges all went dead
speedometer and gas gauge stopped working at the same time
gauges go crazy sometimes, the needles jump all over the place
instrument cluster went completely blank while i was driving
GAUGES NOT WORKING
speedometer needle bounces around and the fuel gauge reads empty on a full tank
dash cluster flickers on and off, some days everything is just dead
tach and speedo quit working but the car drives fine
whole dash display died, no speed no fuel no temp

required_slots.onset_timing: ALL gauges at once vs one gauge vs intermittent flicker — all-at-once points at a fuse/ground/connector; one needle points at that sender or stepper motor [G14]

required_slots.warning_light_behavior: dead/blank vs erratic jumping needles — erratic readings point at the instrument voltage regulator or failing stepper motors [G14]

required_slots.started_when: after a battery change/jump/stereo install vs out of nowhere — disturbed grounds and connectors are common after electrical work

required_slots.drivable_state: driving without a speedometer/fuel gauge — worth prompt scheduling, and note if the cluster died WHILE driving

confusables.q: Is it just the gauge cluster acting up, or are other things (radio, locks, windows) also glitching randomly?
Are the gauges themselves dead or jumping, or is it the dash LIGHTING getting dim/flickering?
Did the gauges stop working, or did a bunch of warning lights come ON with the gauges still reading?

notes: All-gauges-dead simultaneously is usually upstream of the cluster: a blown instrument-panel fuse, a bad ground, or a loose harness connector; erratic/jumping needles point at the instrument voltage regulator, worn stepper motors, or cracked solder joints inside the cluster [G14]. Partially covered before by multiple_random_electrical_glitches — this is the dedicated entry when the CLUSTER is the complaint. If the whole car dies with it (engine stumbles, lights out), that's the charging-system lane (died_while_driving / battery_charging_light), not the cluster. No speedometer is a real usability/safety annoyance — schedule promptly, no safety override.

display: Milky oil / coolant mixing with oil

milky white gunk under my oil cap

antifreeze getting into my oil i think, both look wrong

required_slots.fluid_color: milky/tan/frothy ON THE DIPSTICK vs a light film only under the oil CAP — dipstick-deep milkiness = coolant in the oil; cap-only film on a short-trip winter car is often benign condensation [G15]

required_slots.onset_timing: coolant level dropping and/or oil level RISING with it — the fluid-exchange pair that confirms mixing [G15]

required_slots.drivable_state: how far away they are / still driving daily — milky oil can't lubricate properly; the advice is stop driving it and tow or drive-in immediately-and-short [G15]

required_slots.started_when: after overheating episode / recent head or cooling work vs out of nowhere — an overheat right before is the classic head-gasket story

required_slots.smell_descriptor: any white sweet-smelling exhaust smoke with it — the exhaust side of the same failure

confusables.q: Is the main thing milky oil on the dipstick, or white smoke from the exhaust? (Both together point at the same head-gasket problem — mention both.)
Is the coolant just getting low, or does the OIL also look milky like chocolate milk?
Is it currently overheating, or is the concern what the oil looks like on the dipstick?

notes: The under-the-hood presentation of a head-gasket / oil-cooler / cracked-block failure — the FLU white-smoke entry covers the exhaust side only; this covers the dipstick side. Coolant emulsified in oil ("chocolate milk") destroys the oil's ability to protect bearings — continuing to drive multiplies the damage, hence the flag and the tow-first advice [G15]. Benign look-alike to keep out: a thin milky film under the oil CAP only, on a car doing short cold-weather trips, is usually condensation — the dipstick-vs-cap-only question separates them [G15]. Rising oil level + falling coolant is strong confirmation. Customers frequently self-diagnose "blown head gasket" — keep the observations (milky dipstick, coolant loss, white smoke, overheat history), book the diagnostic.

display: A/C blows warm or hot air (no cooling)

the air conditioning doesnt work
i think the freon is out, no cold air at all
started blowing warm on my way to work and never came back
vents blow fine but the air is just hot

required_slots.total_vs_partial: is the air fully warm/outside-temp or just not cold enough — total loss routes here, partial loss is ac_weak_not_cold_enough

required_slots.onset: sudden vs gradual — sudden points at clutch/electrical/blend door, gradual at slow refrigerant loss

required_slots.recharge_history: any prior AC recharge/service — a recharge that 'wore off' reroutes to the leak concern

required_slots.airflow_ok: is plenty of air coming out (temperature problem) or barely any air (airflow problem) — separates from weak_airflow_from_vents

confusables.q: Is the air totally warm like outside air, or still somewhat cool just not cold enough?
Has it been recharged before and lost its cold again, or is this the first time it stopped cooling?
Is plenty of air coming out but it's warm, or is the air cold but there's barely any of it?
Is it warm on BOTH sides, or is one side cold and the other side warm?

notes: Total cooling loss: low/empty refrigerant, compressor clutch not engaging, electrical, OR a blend door stuck full-hot (air blows genuinely HOT with A/C selected — a common recharge-mis-spend; the charge is often fine) [MACS/ASE-A7]. Does NOT include: partial cooling (ac_weak), heat complaints (no_heat), one-sided cooling (blend-door actuator). A loud squeal/burning-rubber smell at the same moment the AC died suggests a seized compressor shredding the belt — cross-domain to the noise/belt concern and worth advisor attention.

required_slots.speed_dependence: better moving vs at idle/stoplight — idle-only weakness points at condenser airflow/fan

required_slots.weather_dependence: fails only on hot days — classic low-charge signature

required_slots.cycling_pattern: cools then goes warm then cold again — evaporator freeze-up from low charge

required_slots.recharge_history: prior recharge that faded — reroutes to the leak concern

confusables.q: Is there still SOME cool air, or is it completely warm?
Have you had it recharged before and it went weak again?
Is the air cold but there's barely any of it coming out, or plenty of air that just isn't cold?

notes: Partial cooling: low charge, dirty condenser, weak cooling fan, clogged cabin filter [MACS/ASE-A7]. The 'cold on highway, warm at stoplights' and 'useless over 85 degrees' patterns are the two classic low-charge tells. Customers self-diagnose as 'needs a recharge' — accept the symptom, not the diagnosis.

required_slots.recharge_history: when it was last recharged and how long the cold lasted — the recurrence interval is the whole story

required_slots.current_state: blowing warm now vs weak now — sets urgency and the test baseline

required_slots.dye_or_service_history: dye already in the system / prior leak repairs — changes the diagnostic starting point

confusables.q: Has the A/C been recharged before and lost its cold again, or is this the first failure?
Did the cooling fade weeks/months after a recharge, or has it just always been weak?

notes: The SERVICE here is refrigerant leak detection (pressure/dye/electronic), not another recharge [ASE-A7]. Symptom presentation is identical to warm/weak AC — the recharge-recurrence history is what routes here. Customers phrase it as a request ('just needs freon again'); the booking should be a leak test.

required_slots.temp_gauge_state: does the temperature gauge reach normal, stay low, or run high — stays-low points at thermostat (cooling system), normal points at heater core/blend door, high is an overheating concern

required_slots.coolant_history: recently low coolant / topping off — pulls the cause toward the cooling system

required_slots.speed_dependence: warm while moving but cold at idle — low coolant level/flow signature

required_slots.both_sides: cold on both sides vs one side only — one side only is the blend-door concern

confusables.q: Is there no heat on BOTH sides, or does one side blow warm while the other is cold?
Is the temperature gauge or a red temp light reading HIGH, or is it just no warm air with a normal gauge?
Have you had to add coolant/antifreeze, or seen a bright green/orange puddle or a sweet smell?

notes: HVAC owns the no-heat complaint; the CAUSE may be cooling-side (stuck-open thermostat, low coolant) or HVAC-side (heater core clog, blend-door actuator) [MACS/ASE-A7]. The temp GAUGE is the discriminator. If the gauge runs HIGH or coolant is disappearing, this is an engine-cooling/overheating concern (cross-domain), not a comfort issue.

display: Weak or no airflow from vents (blower/fan)

required_slots.airflow_pattern: only-on-high (classic blower resistor) vs weak-everywhere (filter/blower) vs completely dead — each is a different fix

required_slots.temperature_ok: is the air the right temperature, just not enough of it — confirms airflow vs cooling problem

required_slots.onset: sudden death vs gradual weakening vs fades-after-20-minutes (evaporator icing)

confusables.q: Is the air cold but there's barely any of it, or is plenty of air coming out that just isn't cold?
Is there barely any air anywhere, or is plenty of air coming out — just from the wrong vents?
Is the main problem the amount of air, or a noise the fan makes?

notes: Airflow VOLUME concern: blower motor, blower resistor ('only works on high' — high speed bypasses the resistor [MACS/ASE-A7]), clogged cabin filter, debris in the blower. Air temperature is typically fine. Air coming out the WRONG outlets at normal volume is a separate mode-door concern.

display: Air comes out the wrong vents / won't switch
anchors: air only comes out the defrost vents no matter what i pick
stuck blowing on my feet, wont switch to the dash vents

required_slots.which_outlets: where the air IS coming out (defrost/floor/dash) and where it won't go — identifies the stuck mode door

required_slots.volume_normal: airflow amount is normal — confirms distribution (mode door) not volume (blower)

required_slots.accel_dependence: jumps to defrost under hard acceleration — vacuum-actuator loss tell on older vehicles

confusables.q: Is plenty of air coming out but from the wrong vents, or is there barely any air at all?
Is the complaint where the air comes out, or that the windows won't clear?

notes: Mode-door / vacuum-actuator failure — distribution, not volume [MACS/ASE-A7]. Vacuum-operated systems fail-safe to DEFROST, so 'stuck on defrost' plus 'snaps to defrost when I floor it' is the vacuum-leak signature. Temperature and volume are usually fine; customers often misreport it as 'the AC is broken'.

display: One side blows hot, the other cold (dual-zone)

required_slots.which_side: which zone is wrong (driver/passenger/rear) — locates the failed blend-door actuator

required_slots.dial_response: does that side's temperature dial change anything — a dead dial confirms the actuator

required_slots.clicking_noise: tick/click behind the dash when the temp is changed — stripped actuator gears tell

confusables.q: Is it warm on one side only, or on both sides?
Is heat missing on one side only, or everywhere?
Is the main complaint the temperature difference, or the clicking noise itself?

notes: Only possible on dual/tri-zone systems — points squarely at a blend-door actuator on the affected side [MACS]. Applies to heat AND cooling asymmetry. Often accompanied by a clicking/ticking behind the dash (stripped actuator gears). Not a refrigerant problem — one side working proves the charge.

anchors: musty smell like dirty gym socks when i first turn the AC on
AC smells like mildew when it starts up
the vents smell moldy, worse on humid days

required_slots.smell_type: musty/mildew (evaporator growth) vs burning-electrical (blower/resistor) vs sweet-syrup (heater core coolant) — three different problems

required_slots.vent_tied: does it come through the dash vents with the fan on, or from the carpet/seats/trunk — carpet/trunk musty is a water-leak/interior concern, not HVAC

required_slots.timing: worst at first turn-on / on humid days (evaporator signature) vs constant

required_slots.heat_or_ac: with A/C (evaporator) vs with heat (heater core) — picks the failing side

confusables.q: Does the smell come through the dash VENTS with the fan on, or from wet carpet/seats/trunk?
Is the sweet smell blowing through the vents with the heat on, or strongest under the hood or outside the car?
Does the burning smell come through the vents when the fan runs, or from under the hood regardless of the fan?

notes: Musty/dirty-socks at first A/C start = microbial growth on the evaporator, worsened by an overdue cabin filter [MACS]. Sweet/maple-syrup with heat + fogging glass + damp passenger carpet = leaking heater core (coolant — escalate; fumes can cause headaches). Burning electrical through vents = failing blower motor/resistor. Vent-tied smells belong here; carpet/trunk mustiness routes to the water-leak concern.

required_slots.which_glass: front windshield (airflow/AC dehumidify) vs rear window (electrical grid) — completely different systems

required_slots.film_or_residue: greasy/oily film on the inside glass — heater-core leak tell, escalates to a coolant concern

required_slots.carpet_wet: damp carpet or sweet smell alongside — heater core or water leak, not just humidity

required_slots.weather_pattern: worse in rain/cold/with passengers — normal humidity load vs a failed dehumidify (AC compressor)

confusables.q: Is there a sweet smell with the fog, or just fog?
Is the carpet actually wet after rain, or are the windows just fogging?
Is the problem fog on the INSIDE of the glass, or the wipers not clearing the outside?

notes: Persistent inside fog = failed dehumidification (A/C compressor runs in defrost mode — a dead compressor shows up in WINTER as fog [MACS/ASE-A7]), a heater-core leak (greasy film + sweet smell), or trapped moisture from a water leak. Rear-glass grid not heating is electrical, folded here. Visibility-adjacent — treat as prompt, not emergency.

display: Strange noise from the vents / behind the dash
anchors: tick tick tick behind the dash when i change the temperature
clicking noise from the dash every time i start the car

required_slots.noise_type: click/tick (actuator) vs rattle/flap (debris in blower) vs whistle (duct/filter) vs grind-whir (blower bearing) vs slosh-gurgle (clogged evaporator drain)

required_slots.follows_controls: does it change with fan speed / vent mode / the temp dial — confirms HVAC vs an under-hood noise

required_slots.when: at startup, on dial change, on turns (sloshing) — narrows the source

confusables.q: Does the noise change with the FAN speed and vent buttons, or is it a squeal from under the hood when the A/C kicks on?
Is it just the sloshing sound, or is water actually showing up on the floor?
Is the clicking the whole complaint, or is one side also blowing the wrong temperature?

notes: Noise that follows the HVAC controls. Sloshing/gurgling behind the dash = condensate backed up in a clogged evaporator drain — the audible precursor to water dumping on the passenger floor [MACS]. A squeal under the hood on A/C engage is a belt/compressor concern (noise domain), NOT a vent noise.

required_slots.ac_correlation: does the water track A/C use (evaporator drain) vs rain/car wash (body seal/sunroof) — THE routing fact

required_slots.water_character: clear and odorless (condensation) vs sweet/greasy/colored (coolant — heater core, escalate)

required_slots.location_in_cabin: passenger front footwell (classic evaporator drain) vs driver side / rear / headliner (body leak more likely)

confusables.q: Does the floor get wet after running the A/C on dry days, or after rain or a car wash?
Is the liquid clear and odorless like plain water, or does it feel greasy or smell sweet?

notes: Clogged/misrouted evaporator drain dumping condensation into the cabin instead of under the car — a real repair, distinct from the normal clear puddle UNDER the car [MACS; ENGFIX]. Often preceded by a sloshing sound behind the dash. Sweet-smelling or greasy liquid = heater core (coolant) — different problem, escalate.

display: Clear water puddle under the car (A/C condensation)
anchors: small puddle under the passenger side, completely clear, no smell, just like plain water

required_slots.fluid_character: clear + odorless + watery (normal condensation) vs colored/sweet/slick (a real fluid leak — cross-domain)

required_slots.ac_use: appears after A/C or defrost use on warm/humid days — confirms condensation

required_slots.location_under_car: mid/passenger-front (evaporator drain area) vs front-center (radiator area — coolant suspicion) vs elsewhere

confusables.q: Is the puddle clear and odorless like plain water, or bright green/orange/pink or sweet-smelling?
Is the water UNDER the car, or inside on the carpet?

notes: Clear odorless water under the mid/passenger side after A/C use is NORMAL evaporator drainage — a reassurance case, not a repair [ASE-A7]. Kept as an anchor so 'my car is leaking!' panic doesn't misroute to a coolant leak. Any color, sweetness, or slickness reroutes to the fluid-leak domain.

required_slots.rain_correlation: wet after rain/car wash (body leak) vs after A/C use (evaporator drain) — the routing pivot

required_slots.entry_location: headliner/A-pillar (sunroof drains/windshield seal) vs footwell (cowl drain/door seal) vs trunk (tail-lamp/hatch seal) — locates the leak path

required_slots.recent_glass_or_body_work: windshield replacement or body repair just before it started — seal-set suspicion

required_slots.electrical_weirdness: any electrical gremlins alongside — water in a harness/module escalates it

confusables.q: Does it get wet after rain or a car wash, or after running the A/C on a dry day?

Frequently arrives disguised as 'my AC is leaking' — the rain-vs-A/C question is the whole triage. Standing water breeds mold and corrodes under-carpet modules; worth prompt attention.

anchors: loud wind noise from the drivers door on the highway

required_slots.location: which door/window/glass area the noise comes from — locates the failed seal or fitment

required_slots.speed_threshold: the speed it starts at — confirms aerodynamic (wind) vs mechanical noise

required_slots.recent_glass_work: after a windshield or window replacement — glass-set/molding suspicion

required_slots.changes_with_window: does cracking a window or pressing the door panel change it — confirms a seal path

confusables.q: Does it whistle like air and change if you press on the door or crack a window, or is it a hum that just grows with speed?
Does the noise change with the FAN speed, or only with road speed?

notes: Door/window weatherstrip shrinkage, glass sitting proud after an R&R, or mirror/trim turbulence — a body-seal concern, typically an advisor/inspection item rather than a diagnostic test [Duffy, Auto Body Repair Technology via KB]. Wind noise changes with door/window state, not engine load. Cross-domain: speed-proportional HUM belongs to tires/bearings (noise domain).

required_slots.stuck_open_or_closed: won't OPEN vs won't stay CLOSED — a hood or trunk that won't latch shut is a driving hazard

confusables.q: Is it the LOCK that won't lock/unlock, or the latch/handle that won't physically open or close?

The 'does the button click or hum?' question makes the split [HALDERMAN]. A hood that won't fully latch deserves a don't-drive-far caution in notes to the customer.

required_slots.fob_vs_switch: dead from the fob only (fob/receiver) vs dead from fob AND switch (circuit/actuator)

required_slots.rain_correlation: worse after rain or a car wash — water-intrusion corrosion in a door harness or module

required_slots.self_operating: locks cycling on their own — flaky switch/actuator or water gremlin, group with any other random electrical acts

confusables.q: Do the locks work from the switch inside the car but not the remote, or from neither?
Is it just the locks, or are several random electrical things acting up (worse after rain)?
Is the lock the problem, or does the door physically not open/close?

notes: One dead lock = that door's actuator or its in-door harness (water/corrosion-prone) [HALDERMAN; Dorman actuator testing docs via KB]. All-locks-dead = shared fuse/relay/BCM. Locks clicking on their own or acting up after rain = water-intrusion gremlin — if multiple accessories misbehave, that's the broader electrical-gremlin concern.

required_slots.car_still_starts: the car STARTS normally — the fact that keeps this out of the security no-start concern

required_slots.which_functions: lock/unlock vs remote start vs trunk vs proximity range — one function vs all functions splits fob vs receiver

required_slots.battery_tried: fob battery already replaced — rules out the cheapest cause

required_slots.both_fobs: does the spare fob behave the same — same on both fobs points at the car's receiver, not the fob

confusables.q: Does the car START fine and only the remote is acting up, or does the car NOT start?

notes: Fob battery, keyless receiver/antenna, or lost pairing [HALDERMAN]. Aftermarket remote-start systems are a separate installed system — don't collapse 'remote start quit' into the OE fob without asking. Intermittent 'key not detected' that still starts = weak fob battery, still this concern. If the car won't start at all, that's the security/immobilizer no-start.

display: Won't start — security / key not recognized
anchors: car cranks but wont start and the little security light is flashing
wont start, the key light is blinking on the dash
anti theft light flashing and it just cranks and cranks
says key not detected and it wont start at all
immobilizer wont recognize my key, tried the spare too
theft light comes on and the engine wont even turn over
the key wont turn in the ignition at all
my key is stuck in the ignition and wont come out
car with a lock symbol on the dash and it wont start
wont start unless i lock and unlock the doors with the key first, some security thing
battery is fine, lights are bright, but it wont fire and the security light keeps blinking

required_slots.security_light_state: a flashing security/theft/key light during the no-start — the immobilizer-actively-blocking tell [YOURMECH]

required_slots.crank_behavior: cranks-but-won't-fire (fuel/spark withheld) vs no crank at all (starter-cut design or a battery problem) — shapes the diagnosis

required_slots.battery_health: lights bright, no jump needed — a normal battery is what separates this from a dead-battery no-start

required_slots.spare_key_tried: does a spare key/fob start it — spare works = bad key/fob; both fail = antenna ring/module

required_slots.key_mechanical: key won't turn / key stuck in ignition — the mechanical cylinder/interlock cousins live here too

confusables.q: Are the dash lights bright and the battery fine with a security/key light flashing, or are the lights dim/dead and it clicks or needed a jump?
Does the car not START, or does it start fine and only the remote functions are broken?
Is the car refusing to start right now, or do you just need a key made or programmed?

notes: Immobilizer withholds fuel/spark (or cuts the starter) when the transponder isn't recognized — flashing security light with a NORMAL battery is the signature [HALDERMAN; YOURMECH; toyotanation.com forum pattern]. Common causes: dead fob battery on push-button cars, damaged transponder key, weak antenna ring, aftermarket alarm/remote-start wiring. Key-won't-turn and key-stuck-in-ignition are mechanical cylinder/shift-interlock cousins, same booking. Customer is usually stranded — offer the tow path. A blinking security light while PARKED is the normal armed indicator, not a fault.

display: Security / anti-theft light on (car runs fine)
anchors: anti theft light stays on while im driving but the car runs fine
security light came on and hasnt gone off
little car with a key symbol lit up on the dash
SECURITY LIGHT ON
lock symbol stays lit after i start the car
anti-theft light on, starts and drives normal though
the immobilizer light wont go out
security light blinks when the car is parked, is that normal?

required_slots.starts_and_runs: car starts/runs normally — separates this from the security no-start

required_slots.light_behavior: steady while driving (stored fault) vs blinking while parked (NORMAL armed indicator — reassurance, not a repair)

required_slots.other_lights: is it the only light on — several lights at once is a different (charging-cascade) concern

required_slots.alarm_behavior: any alarm/horn misbehavior alongside — pairs with the alarm concern

confusables.q: Does the car start and drive fine with the light on, or does it not start?
Is the security light the only one on, or are several dash lights lit at once?
Is it just the light, or is the alarm/horn actually sounding on its own?

notes: A steady security light while DRIVING = stored anti-theft fault (buckle in the chain: key reader, ajar switch, module). A blinking light while parked/off is the normal armed indicator — reassurance case [YOURMECH; HALDERMAN]. If it escalates to no-start events, route to the security no-start concern.

display: Alarm goes off by itself / won't disarm
anchors: the alarm goes off randomly in the middle of the night
car alarm keeps going off for no reason
horn keeps honking on its own, i had to pull the fuse to make it stop
alarm wont shut off even with the fob
the panic alarm sets itself off when it rains
alarm goes off every time i unlock the door with the key instead of the remote
horn honked by itself in the parking lot until the battery about died
car alarm went off three times last night, my neighbors are furious
ALARM SOUNDING RANDOMLY

required_slots.trigger_pattern: random/at night/in rain/when unlocking with the key — rain points at a wet ajar switch, key-unlock points at a disarm-input fault

required_slots.car_starts_fine: the car starts and drives — keeps this apart from the immobilizer no-start

required_slots.aftermarket_alarm: factory system vs an installed aftermarket alarm/remote start — aftermarket wiring is a top culprit

required_slots.customer_disabled: did they pull a fuse/disconnect the horn — a disabled safety device worth noting on the RO

confusables.q: Does the car start and run fine but the alarm/horn goes off on its own, or does the car NOT start?
Is the alarm actually sounding, or is it just the security light staying on?

notes: The audible perimeter system, distinct from the silent immobilizer. Usual cause: a flaky normally-closed ajar switch (door/hood/trunk) or worn courtesy-light switch feeding a false 'opened' signal; aftermarket alarm wiring is the other frequent culprit [HALDERMAN; freeasestudyguides.com]. Rain-triggered alarms point at a water-soaked switch. Electrical diagnostic booking.

display: Need a key / fob made or programmed
anchors: lost my only key, need a new one made
need a spare key cut and programmed
