need my remote reprogrammed after i changed its battery
can you program a key fob for a 2018 civic
my key is all chewed up and barely turns, want a new one cut

required_slots.keys_remaining: all keys lost vs wants a spare — all-keys-lost is a bigger (immobilizer-programming, possibly tow-in) job

required_slots.key_type: basic metal key vs transponder chip key vs smart/proximity fob — completely different cost and equipment

required_slots.customer_supplied_part: customer already bought a fob/blank — shop policy on programming customer parts applies

confusables.q: Is this a request for a new/spare key, or is the car refusing to start with the key you have?
Do you need a NEW key/fob, or is your existing remote misbehaving?

notes: A service REQUEST, not a symptom — routes to advisor/quote (many general shops sublet all-keys-lost jobs to a locksmith or dealer). 'Key is worn and barely turns' sits on the boundary: still a request while it works, becomes the security no-start concern when it won't turn at all.

display: Driver-assist warning or feature not working (lane/collision/cruise/blind spot/parking)
anchors: lane departure warning light stays on
message says front radar obstruction, driving aids unavailable
pre collision system warning came on and wont go away
adaptive cruise control stopped working and theres a warning about it
blind spot light on the mirror stays lit all the time now
collision mitigation problem and road departure problem both popped up at once
after the snowstorm it said forward driving aids temporarily disabled
little car with sensor waves light came on
parking sensors beep nonstop even with nothing around
lane keep assist unavailable message every morning
cruise wont set and the dash shows some radar error

required_slots.which_feature: the assist feature named — lane keep, pre-collision/AEB, adaptive cruise, blind spot, parking sensors — the customer's words carry it

required_slots.message_behavior: comes-and-goes/self-clears (obstruction) vs steady (fault or calibration) vs several assist messages at once (shared camera/radar)

required_slots.weather_trigger: appeared after snow/ice/mud/heavy rain and clears when cleaned — benign sensor blockage [NISSAN-TSB]

required_slots.recent_service: windshield/alignment/bumper/battery work just before — reroutes to the calibration concern

confusables.q: Did the warning start right after a windshield replacement, alignment, bumper repair, or battery change?
Does the message name a driver-assist feature (lane, collision, cruise, blind spot), or is it the check-engine light?
Are the messages all about driving-assist features, or is the whole dash lit up (battery, ABS, airbag) with the car running rough?
Is a warning or assist feature involved, or is a screen/camera display simply dead with no message?

notes: Covers obstruction messages (snow/ice/mud on the radar/camera — often self-clearing [NISSAN-TSB]), real sensor/module faults, and several assist systems down together (one shared windshield camera feeds lane-keep, collision braking, and road-departure on many cars, so one fault throws multiple messages [Honda Sensing docs via KB]). Base brakes/steering are unaffected — only the assist is paused; reassure but book a scan. An all-ADAS message set is NOT the charging-cascade 'all my dash lights are on' concern. Plain set-speed cruise dying on an older car with no radar is an electrical accessory concern, not ADAS — ask before assuming.

display: Car brakes or steers by itself (false activation)
anchors: car slammed on the brakes by itself, nothing was in front of me
the emergency braking goes off for no reason
collision warning beeps at me when nothings there
lane assist keeps yanking the steering wheel on the highway
car brakes by itself sometimes, kinda scary
the self braking freaks out at overpasses and shadows
auto brake grabbed hard at a plastic bag in the road, almost got rear ended
cruise randomly slams the brakes when the car ahead is way far up
the steering keeps tugging me toward the shoulder for no reason

required_slots.intervention_type: braking vs steering tug vs warning-only beeps — which system is false-firing

required_slots.self_initiated: the CAR did it with no pedal/wheel input — the fact that separates this from a brake or alignment problem

required_slots.conditions: highway/overpasses/shadows/rain — classic phantom-trigger contexts, useful for the diagnosis

required_slots.any_message: an assist warning/message alongside — links it to a specific feature

confusables.q: Does the car brake ITSELF when nothing is in front, or does the pedal feel wrong when YOU press it?
Does a driving-assist system tug the wheel now and then (sometimes with a lane message), or does the car steadily pull to one side all the time?
Is the system actually braking/steering on its own, or just showing a warning?

notes: False AEB/lane-keep activation — the subject of formal NHTSA defect investigations (PE22-003 Honda inadvertent AEB; EA22-002 Tesla phantom braking) [NHTSA-ODI]. Unexpected self-braking at speed is a rear-end-collision hazard: advise promptly and offer to disable/limit use per the owner's manual until diagnosed. Do NOT misroute as a brake-feel or alignment concern — the intervention is self-initiated.

display: Camera/sensor calibration after windshield, alignment, or repair
anchors: just got a new windshield and now the lane assist light is on
windshield was replaced and now the lane keeping pulls me to the left
the glass shop said the camera needs to be calibrated, can you do that?
got an alignment and the lane keep warning popped up right after
do i need the camera recalibrated after a windshield replacement?
cruise control and collision warning quit right after my windshield got replaced
put in a new battery and now the lane keep assist light is on
bumper got repaired and now the parking sensors act up
dealer says i need an adas calibration, do you guys do that?

required_slots.triggering_service: windshield replacement, alignment, suspension work, bumper/sensor R&R, collision repair, or battery replacement — each is a known calibration/relearn trigger [AAA-ADAS; ICAR]

required_slots.symptom_since: warning light vs feature misbehaving (false pulls) vs pure inquiry ('do I need it?') — shapes booking vs quote

required_slots.who_did_the_work: this shop vs elsewhere — a warranty/comeback conversation if it was here

confusables.q: Did this start right after glass/alignment/body/battery work, or out of nowhere?
Is it the lane-assist system steering you (with an assist light/message, after the work), or does the car pull even with all the assists off?

notes: Forward cameras aim THROUGH the windshield and radars through the bumper — calibration is required whenever the glass, sensor, bracket, alignment, or ride height changes, and a battery disconnect can drop the steering-angle/camera relearn [AAA-ADAS; ICAR; SAFELITE]. Real-world forum pattern: glass shops skip or botch the calibration and lane-keep faults or false pulls follow [F150FORUM; subaruoutback.org]. Includes the pure inquiry ('do I need it?') — a quote/advisor conversation. Calibration itself may be sublet; the booking is verification + scan.

display: Seat belt won't latch, retract, or release
anchors: seat belt wont pull out, its stuck
the buckle wont click anymore
seat belt wont retract, just hangs there all loose
drivers seatbelt is stuck and wont come across me
belt latches but then pops back open
the seat belt in the back wont buckle for my kids car seat
seat belt is frayed and keeps getting caught
SEAT BELT STUCK

required_slots.which_seat: which position — driver/passenger/rear; a rear belt used for a car seat raises urgency

required_slots.failure_type: won't pull out (retractor lock) vs won't latch (buckle) vs won't retract (spring) vs frayed webbing — different hardware

required_slots.dash_light: no SRS light involved — a lit airbag light moves it to the airbag_srs_light concern

confusables.q: Is a warning light on the dash involved, or is it purely the belt hardware sticking?

notes: Mechanical restraint hardware (retractor/buckle/webbing) with NO dash light — distinct from the SRS electrical system. A belt that locks when yanked fast is normal (inertia lock — pull slowly). A belt that won't latch means someone rides unprotected: schedule promptly, and note that pretensioner-equipped belts after a crash are a restraint-system repair, not a lube job [ASE-A6 via KB].

display: Hybrid/EV won't power on / won't go to READY
anchors: my hybrid wont start, the dash lights up but it wont go to ready
prius wont start this morning, jumped the little 12 volt battery and it fired right up
pushed the power button and nothing happens, the screen comes on but the car is dead
my leaf wouldnt power on, had to jump the 12v and then it was fine
hybrid wont go into ready mode and theres warning lights everywhere
the ev is completely dead in the garage, wont turn on at all
no crank no nothing — its a hybrid so i guess theres no starter sound anyway
power button does nothing unless i hold the key fob right against it
my plug in hybrid wont turn on after sitting for two weeks

required_slots.powertrain_stated: customer says hybrid/EV/plug-in — a model name alone (prius, leaf) should be CONFIRMED, not assumed

required_slots.ready_vs_crank: won't reach READY / no crank sound (normal for EVs — there's no starter to hear) vs an engine physically cranking — separates this from an ICE no-start

required_slots.jump_effect: jumping the small 12V battery fixed it (even temporarily) — the strongest evidence it's the cheap 12V, not the traction pack

required_slots.sat_how_long: sat days/weeks (12V self-drain) vs died mid-day — onset context

required_slots.hybrid_warning_present: a turtle/red-triangle/check-hybrid message alongside — pulls it toward the HV-fault concerns

confusables.q: Is it a hybrid or electric — does it fail to reach READY with no cranking, or is it a gas car that clicks or cranks slowly?
Did jumping the small 12-volt battery bring it back, or is there a red triangle / check-hybrid-system warning that stays on?
Does the car not power ON, or does it power on fine but won't CHARGE when plugged in?

notes: The 12V auxiliary battery must wake the computers that close the high-voltage contactors — a flat 12V blocks READY even with a perfectly healthy traction pack [DOE-AFDC]. Rule the cheap 12V out FIRST; never tell a customer their 'hybrid battery' died from this symptom alone (a four-figure fear with a $200 usual answer) [PHXHYB]. There is no crank sound to report on an EV — absence of cranking is normal, not a clue. Stranded customers: offer the tow path; hybrids can often be 12V-jumped per the owner's manual.

display: Hybrid/EV reduced power / turtle mode / limp
anchors: my electric car suddenly lost power and a turtle light came on
the ev is in turtle mode, wont go over 40
hybrid went into limp mode on the highway, could barely keep going
reduced power warning and now it crawls up hills
car cut its power way back and the hybrid warning came on
leaf is showing a turtle and has zero acceleration
power limited message on my ev and its just creeping
hybrid has no power all of a sudden and theres a warning light on
it maxes out at like 25 now, some kind of power limit thing

required_slots.powertrain_stated: hybrid/EV stated — the same words from a gas car are an ICE low-power concern (different domain)

required_slots.warning_shown: turtle icon / reduced-power message / hybrid warning — confirms a deliberate derate vs a mechanical problem

required_slots.conditions: very hot day, very cold pack, or battery nearly empty — the three documented derate triggers [NISSAN-LEAF]

required_slots.still_moving: crawling but moving vs stopped/stranded — sets tow vs drive-in

confusables.q: Is the vehicle a hybrid or fully electric, and is there a turtle / reduced-power / hybrid warning showing?
Is the car actually slowed to a crawl, or is it mainly the warning message with normal driving?
Does it still drive (just weak), or won't it power on at all?

notes: A deliberate protective derate: the car limits traction-motor power when the pack/inverter is too hot or cold, charge is extremely low, or a fault is detected [NISSAN-LEAF; DOE-AFDC]. Flagged advise_immediately because a car capped at crawl speed on a highway is a live hazard — advise pulling off and offer tow/priority booking. Needs HV-aware diagnosis, not a generic engine code scan.

display: Check Hybrid System / red triangle warning
anchors: red triangle with an exclamation point came on in my prius
check hybrid system message came up, is it safe to drive?
hybrid system warning light came on out of nowhere
CHECK ENGINE LIGHT ON, message service high voltage charging system
dash says hybrid system malfunction, stop the vehicle
the triangle of death popped up on my dash
warning says check hybrid system and the car feels weaker
hybrid warning light and the check engine light both on
master warning light and a hybrid system message together

required_slots.message_text: the exact message ('check hybrid system', 'service high voltage charging system', red triangle) — the customer's words carry the routing

required_slots.drive_impact: driving normally vs weak vs won't move — normal-driving books urgent, crawl/no-move goes to the reduced-power/tow path

required_slots.other_lights: CEL or master warning alongside — common with HV faults, still routes here on a hybrid

required_slots.powertrain_stated: confirm hybrid/EV — a bare red triangle on a model-name-only report should be confirmed before routing HV

confusables.q: Does the message mention the hybrid system or high voltage, or is it just the check-engine light?
Is the car still driving normally with the warning, or has it lost power?
Is the warning on while the car runs, or will the car not power on at all?

notes: The hybrid master warning (Toyota/Lexus 'red triangle of death', 'Check Hybrid System') signals a fault in an HV subsystem — traction battery, inverter, HV cooling, motor-generator, or DC/DC [DOE-AFDC; PHXHYB]. Red master warning = advise immediately per the red-light rule; some faults shut the car down shortly after. Customers file it as a check-engine light — it needs HV-capable diagnosis, and shops without an HV tech should say so up front (advisor conversation). Never suggest DIY around orange HV cabling.

anchors: my ev wont charge at home anymore and the port light is red
the charger clicks but nothing happens
plugged in my plug-in hybrid overnight and it didnt charge at all
car stops charging after a few minutes every time
the charge port wont lock onto the plug
it only charges to 80 percent then quits
fast charging doesnt work but charging at home is fine
getting a check charging system message when i plug it in
the charge cable is stuck in the port and wont release

required_slots.where_it_fails: home charger vs public fast charger vs both — both-fail points at the car, one-fails points at that charger

required_slots.failure_moment: won't start charging (handshake/port lock) vs stops mid-charge vs stops at a set percent (often a user-set charge LIMIT — normal, reassure)

required_slots.port_indicators: port light color / error message — the car is usually telling you which side failed

required_slots.cable_stuck: cable locked in the port — a today-problem needing the manual-release walkthrough

confusables.q: Does the car drive fine and just won't CHARGE, or won't it power on at all?
Is this about plugging the car in to charge, or a battery warning light while driving?

notes: On-board charger, charge-port/EVSE handshake, or BMS fault [DOE-AFDC]. 'Stops at 80%' is usually a user-set charge limit — reassurance, not repair. Many general shops decline EVSE/HV charging work: this concern should surface to an advisor (sublet/dealer referral) rather than force a generic diagnostic. Try-the-other-charger is the free first test to suggest.

display: Hybrid/EV battery losing range or capacity
anchors: my hybrid battery isnt holding a charge and the gas mileage tanked
electric range dropped from 200 to like 130
prius mpg went from 45 to 30, i think the battery is dying
the battery bars dropped on my dash and im losing range
hybrid battery dies fast and the engine runs constantly now
how much to test a hybrid battery? mine seems weak
range keeps getting worse every month
the car barely goes into electric mode anymore

required_slots.magnitude_and_timeline: how much range/mpg lost over how long — gradual fade vs a sudden drop (sudden suggests a fault, not aging)

required_slots.any_warning: hybrid warning/CEL alongside — a lit warning moves it to the hybrid-warning concern

required_slots.weather_context: winter range loss is normal physics — a seasonal drop needs a season-over-season comparison, not a battery

required_slots.vehicle_age_miles: pack age/mileage — frames the aging-vs-defect and warranty conversation (many HV packs carry 8yr/100k+ warranties)

confusables.q: Is a hybrid warning light on, or is it just gradual range/mileage loss with no light?
Does it charge fine but not go as far, or is the problem charging itself?

notes: Gradual traction-pack capacity fade is a normal lithium-ion aging pattern [DOE-AFDC]; cold weather alone cuts EV range substantially (reassurance path in winter). A capacity test is an HV-shop job — advisor/quote conversation, and check the HV-battery warranty before quoting repairs. An engine that suddenly runs constantly on a hybrid pairs with pack weakness — same booking.

anchors: my hybrid brakes feel weird and grabby first thing on cold mornings
the brakes on my ev feel different, like they let go for a second then grab
braking feels jerky when the battery is full
the brake pedal on my prius feels notchy and inconsistent
regen braking feels weaker in the winter
one pedal driving isnt slowing the car down like it used to
brakes grab hard going downhill right after a full charge
pedal feels spongy sometimes then normal — its a hybrid if that matters

required_slots.powertrain_stated: hybrid/EV stated — the same words from a gas car are a straight brake concern (brakes domain)

required_slots.condition_pattern: cold mornings / full battery / downhill after charging — the regen-limited contexts where blending changes feel [BOSCH]

required_slots.noise_present: any grinding/squealing — noise means friction-brake wear, route to the brakes domain regardless of powertrain

required_slots.constant_vs_conditional: feel change only in those conditions (regen blending, often normal) vs all the time (book a brake inspection)

confusables.q: Does the odd feel only show up cold or with a full battery on your hybrid/EV, or is it there all the time / with grinding or squealing?
Is it the braking feel, or the car's power/acceleration that changed?

notes: Regen/friction blending: when the pack is cold or full it can't accept charge, so the car hands braking to the friction brakes and the pedal feel/deceleration curve changes — frequently NORMAL [BOSCH; DOE-AFDC]. Book a brake inspection when it's constant, noisy, or the customer feels unsafe; otherwise this is largely an education/reassurance conversation. Any grinding/squeal/pulsation = ordinary friction-brake concern (brakes domain).

anchors: transmission is slipping
when driving up a hill the trans slips
engine revs high but the car barely picks up speed, especially on hills
revs up but doesnt go
rpms jump way up before it finally catches
tranny slipping bad, revs but no power
feels like its slipping when i accelerate
gas pedal down, motor screams, car barely moves
feels like the emergency brake is on when i give it gas
revs up between gears then catches
speedometer stays the same while the rpms shoot up
transmision keeps sliping in and out of gear
slips when its hot out or after driving a while

required_slots.transmission_behavior: what the trans is DOING (slipping = rpm outruns road speed) — the single fact that separates slip from engine low-power, limp mode, and no-engagement

required_slots.onset_timing: when it slips (accelerating, uphill, under load, when warm) — load/heat dependence points at fluid or clutch-pack wear

required_slots.drivable_state: still moves vs barely moves vs needs tow — severe slip becomes a tow, changes booking urgency

required_slots.started_when: sudden vs gradual — gradual worsening is classic wear; sudden points at fluid loss

required_slots.fluid_leak_seen: any red puddle noticed — low ATF is the #1 cheap cause worth capturing up front

confusables.q: When it feels weak, does the engine rev up HIGHER than normal while the car doesn't speed up — or do the revs stay low and it just feels gutless?
Is your car an automatic or a stick shift?
Does it still move (just weakly), or did it suddenly stop moving at all — maybe with a loud bang?
Does it move but rev high without pulling, or is it capped at a certain speed / stuck in one gear with warning lights on?

notes: Customers almost never say 'transmission' correctly — the #1 misattribution is 'the engine has no power' (dossier M1). Includes flare-between-shifts ('revs up between gears then catches'). Does NOT include: manual clutch slip (its own entry), rpm-capped limp mode (stuck_in_gear_limp_mode), snapped axle 'revs but goes nowhere' (axle_broke_wont_move). Fact check: revs-outrun-speed = slip [Halderman 6e via KB; justanswer.com 'starts up but just revs'].

anchors: shifts hard, slams into gear
the car jerks while switching gears in the lower gears
hard shift between second and third
bangs into gear when i put it in drive
jerks really hard when i put it in reverse
clunks every time it changes gears
transmission shifts late and then hits hard
feels like somebody rear-ended me when it shifts
shifting has been getting rougher and rougher
jerky shifting when the car is cold
slams from 1st to 2nd

required_slots.transmission_behavior: harsh/hard shift vs delayed vs slip — names the failure mode (valve body / solenoid pressure fault vs engagement problem)

required_slots.onset_timing: which shift or action triggers it (specific gear change, into D/R, cold only) — a single-gear hard shift points at one solenoid/circuit

required_slots.warning_light_behavior: check-engine or transmission light present — a stored code changes the diagnostic path

required_slots.started_when: sudden after a battery change / jump (lost shift adapts) vs gradual wear

required_slots.recent_action: recent service, battery disconnect, or fluid change right before it started — adaptive-learn resets and wrong fluid are common triggers

confusables.q: Does the jerk happen exactly WHEN the transmission changes gears, or does it buck and stumble while you're cruising at a steady speed?
Is it a jolt with every gear change while driving, or a single clunk from under the car right when you first put it in Drive or Reverse?
Does it shift too HARD, or does it pause a few seconds before it engages at all?

notes: Covers hard 1-2/2-3 shifts and bang into D/R. 'Jerks while switching gears' is a documented dual-read (harsh shift OR engine buck under load — KB auto-trans M5); the discriminator question resolves it. Does NOT include: single take-off clunk (driveline backlash), pops OUT of gear (own entry).

display: Delay going into Drive or Reverse
anchors: takes a few seconds to move after i put it in drive
car hesitates when putting in gear after sitting for awhile
put it in drive and it just sits there, then finally grabs
shift into reverse and nothing happens for a couple seconds then it clunks in
in the morning its like its in neutral for a second before it goes
slow to engage when cold, fine once its warmed up
wont engage until i tap the gas, then it drives fine
felt like it was in neutral
long pause between park and drive
takes forever to go into gear first thing in the morning

required_slots.transmission_behavior: delayed engagement (pause then normal drive) vs no engagement at all — severity fork

required_slots.onset_timing: on gear SELECTION from park vs while already moving — the fact that separates this from engine hesitation on acceleration

required_slots.weather_or_temp_condition: cold-start / after sitting vs all the time — cold-only delay is classic seal leak-down and less urgent [toyotanation.com, forum.ih8mud.com threads]

required_slots.drivable_state: still drives normally after it catches — confirms it's delay, not failure

required_slots.started_when: getting longer over time — trend indicates worsening internal leak-down

confusables.q: Is the delay when you FIRST put it in Drive or Reverse from Park, or while you're already moving and press the gas?
Does it eventually catch and drive normally, or does it never move no matter how long you wait?

notes: Classic phrasing is time-of-day framed ('in the morning', 'after sitting'). Cause set: internal leak-down / worn seals / low fluid [Sun Devil Auto; Halderman via KB M4]. Does NOT include: hesitation/lag while moving (engine driveability domain), stuck shifter (interlock, wont_move entry notes).

display: Starts but won't move / won't go into gear
anchors: car starts but wont move
vehicle starts but does not shift into gear
put it in drive and nothing happens
wont go into gear, had to get it towed
transmission went out, revs but wont move at all
drives fine going forward but wont move in reverse
no reverse all of a sudden
gets stuck in park, have to go back and forth with the shifter to get into gear
shifter moves but the car doesnt do anything
wont come out of park
tow in, does not shift, transmission failed

required_slots.transmission_behavior: no engagement vs one-direction-only (no reverse) vs shifter-stuck — three different repairs behind one customer phrase

required_slots.noise_descriptor: hum/roar (bearing) vs whine/howl (gear mesh) — coarse split between bearing and differential

required_slots.steering_load_sensitivity: louder swerving one way, quieter the other = wheel bearing (weight transfer); constant = tires/diff

required_slots.throttle_phase: on-throttle howl = ring & pinion mesh; coast-only whirr = pinion bearings; constant = wheel bearing/tires — the load-phase names the part

required_slots.warning_light_named: ABS/traction light with a growling wheel — modern hub bearings carry the wheel-speed sensor; one fault, not two

confusables.q: Does the hum get louder when you gently swerve one way and quieter the other, or does it stay the same no matter how you steer?
Does the whine track your ROAD speed (same at a given mph in every gear), or does it change when the transmission shifts?
Is there a humming or growling wheel noise ALONG with the ABS/traction light, or just the light by itself?
Is it mainly a NOISE, or a shake you feel in the seat and floor?

notes: Three mechanisms share one customer sound: wheel/hub bearing (steering-load sensitive, 'snow tires'/'airplane'), diff gear mesh (accel howl), pinion bearings (coast-only whirr) [West Coast Differentials; Halderman via KB 3.5/3.8]. A failing hub bearing can set the ABS/traction light by itself — sensor is integral to the bearing [Tomorrow's Technician]. Cross-domain: wheel bearing itself is owned by the wheels/tires/bearings domain; diff whine is owned here — synthesis should keep both homes and share the discriminators.

confusables.q: Is it a click-click-click SOUND, or the whole vehicle BINDING and hopping like it's fighting itself?

notes: Driveline windup: part-time 4WD on dry pavement can't relieve front/rear speed difference in a turn — bind, hop, 'crow hop' [Halderman via KB 3.9]. The look-alike is limited-slip clutch chatter on a single-axle RWD ('banging, clunking or chattering only on corners' — worn posi clutches or wrong/depleted friction-modifier additive) [West Coast Differentials]. The posi variant is a much cheaper fluid-service fix — the powertrain question is worth real money.
